title: Catapodium tenellum
taxonomy: Catapodium tenellum is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. This species is further grouped into the subfamily Pooideae, which is one of the two subfamilies within the Poaceae family. The genus Catapodium is part of the tribe Poeae, and it is closely related to other genera such as Coleanthus and Koeleria. Within the tribe Poeae, Catapodium tenellum is placed in the subtribe Poinae. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics among different species within the Poaceae family.

intro: Catapodium tenellum, also known as slender barley-grass or slender cat's-foot, is a small, annual grass species native to the Mediterranean region. The health benefits of Catapodium tenellum include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy for gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Catapodium tenellum include teas, infusions, and capsules, often used to treat digestive problems and promote overall well-being.
